g.text: EXPLAIN Output — Table Scan vs Index Scan
Est. table scan cost: 25,000 page reads
Est. index scan cost: 2,500,006 page reads
VERDICT: table scan wins (index too expensive)
Est. table scan cost: 25,000 page reads
Est. index scan cost: 6 page reads
VERDICT: index scan wins (6 vs 25,000 pages)
Pages read: 25,000 (1M rows / 40 rows per page)
Filter work: Column + Ne comparison x 1,000,000
Random I/O penalty: 5x factor applied in planner

div.p: SELECT name FROM users WHERE email = 'alice@example.com'; -- ~0.0001% match: 1 row in 1M-row table -- 1M distinct values -> selectivity = 1/1,000,000

0 OpenTable 0 0 cursor 0 = table 'users' 1 Rewind 0 7 seek to first row; empty->7 2 Column 0 2 r1 = users.gender 3 String8 0 2 r2 = literal 'M' 4 Ne 1 6 if r1 != r2 skip to Next(6) 5 ResultRow 3 5 emit columns [0..4] 6 Next 0 2 advance cursor; more->addr 2 7 Halt 0 0 end program Pattern: Rewind + Next loop over ALL rows No index opened. Sequential page access. Est. rows examined : 1,000,000 Est. rows returned : 500,000 Est. cost : 25,000 page reads

0 OpenTable 0 0 cursor 0 = table 'users' 1 OpenIndex 1 0 cursor 1 = idx_users_email 2 String8 0 2 r2 = 'alice@example.com' 3 SeekGE 1 11 descend index to key >= r2 4 IdxGT 1 11 if index key > r2 stop->11 5 IndexRowid 1 4 r4 = rowid from index leaf 6 SeekRowid 0 10 descend table to rowid r4 7 Column 0 1 r5 = users.name 8 ResultRow 5 1 emit column [name] 9 Next 1 4 advance index cursor; more->4 10 Goto 0 4 (loop back) 11 Halt 0 0 end program Pattern: SeekGE/IdxGT + per-row Rowid lookup Double tree descent per result row. Est. rows examined : 1 Est. rows returned : 1 Est. cost : 6 page reads (3 index + 3 table)
